NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED OF STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT KYO-YA COMPANY, LTD

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS KYO-YA COMPANY, LTD, STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC' NOTICE OF HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION AND AN IME IN CALIFORNIA IS DENIED. THE FACTORS SET FORTH IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2025.260 DO NOT OUTWEIGH SECTION 2025.250'S PRESUMPTION THAT THE DEPOSITION OF A NATURAL PERSON SHALL BE TAKEN WITHIN 75 MILES OF HIS RESIDENCE. PLAINTIFF DID NOT SELECT THE FORUM, STRICTLY SPEAKING, AS HE CANNOT BE SAID TO HAVE CHOSEN TO SUSTAIN AN ALLEGED INJURY WHILE VISITING ANOTHER CITY. THERE IS NO REASON TO BELIEVE PLAINTIFF WILL NOT BE AVAILABLE TO TESTIFY AT TRIAL. AS POINTED OUT BY PLAINTIFF, IT WOULD CAUSE INCONVENIENCE TO PLAINTIFF TO TRAVEL TO SAN FRANCISCO FOR A DEPOSITION. IT IS NOT FEASIBLE TO CONDUCT PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION BY WRITTEN QUESTIONS OR TO OBTAIN THE SAME INFORMATION THROUGH OTHER METHODS OF DISCOVERY. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS POINT OUT THAT OTHER WITNESSES MAY HAVE TO TRAVEL TO SAN FRANCISCO, DEFENDANTS DO NOT EXPLAIN WHO THEY ARE, AND IF DEFENDANT REFER TO PLAINTIFF'S TREATING PHYSICIANS, THOSE WITNESSES WOULD HAVE TO BE DEPOSED IN AUSTIN ANYWAY (AND COULD BE DEPOSED AT THE SAME TIME AS PLAINTIFF) AS THE COURT CANNOT COMPEL THEIR APPEARANCE IN CALIFORNIA. FINALLY, THERE IS NO DIFFERENCE BETWEEN THE EXPENSE OF REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO TRAVEL TO SAN FRANCISCO AND THAT OF REQUIRING DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL TO TRAVEL TO AUSTIN. FURTHERMORE, AS POINTED OUT BY PLAINTIFF, THERE IS NO STATUTORY PROVISION FOR REQUIRING A PLAINTIFF IN A PERSONAL INJURY ACTION TO SUBMIT TO A PHYSICAL EXAMINATION MORE THAN 75 MILES FROM HIS OR HER RESIDENCE. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. 2032.220, SUBD. (A)(2).) THE ONLY STATUTORY PROVISION ALLOWING EXAMINATIONS OF ANY KIND TO BE CONDUCTED MORE THAN 75 MILES FROM THE RESIDENCE OF THE EXAMINEE PERTAINS TO EXAMINATIONS OTHER THAN THOSE OF A PLAINTIFF IN A PERSONAL INJURY ACTION. ( 2032.310, SUBD. (A); 2032.320, SUBD. (E).)

Ntc Of Hearing On Deft'S Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Deposition And An Ime In California
Off Calendar Oct-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Summer M. Smith, Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, 650.780.1614, appearing for the Defendants;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Ruling: THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS KYO-YA COMPANY, LTD, STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC' Notice Of Hearing On Defendant's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Deposition And An IME In California is DENIED. The factors set forth in Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.260 do not outweigh section 2025.250's presumption that the deposition of a natural person shall be taken within 75 miles of his residence. Plaintiff did not select the forum, strictly speaking, as he cannot be said to have chosen to sustain an alleged injury while visiting another city. There is no reason to believe Plaintiff will not be available to testify at trial. As pointed out by Plaintiff, it would cause inconvenience to Plaintiff to travel to San Francisco for a deposition. It is not feasible to conduct Plaintiff's deposition by written questions or to obtain the same information through other methods of discovery. Although Defendants point out that other witnesses may have to travel to San Francisco, Defendants do not explain who they are, and if Defendant refer to Plaintiff's treating physicians, those witnesses would have to be deposed in Austin anyway (and could be deposed at the same time as Plaintiff) as the Court cannot compel their appearance in California. Finally, there is no difference between the expense of requiring Plaintiff to travel to San Francisco and that of requiring Defendants' counsel to travel to Austin. Furthermore, as pointed out by Plaintiff, there is no statutory provision for requiring a plaintiff in a personal injury action to submit to a physical examination more than 75 miles from his or her residence. (See Code Civ. Proc. 2032.220, subd. (a)(2).) The only statutory provision allowing examinations of any kind to be conducted more than 75 miles from the residence of the examinee pertains to examinations other than those of a plaintiff in a personal injury action. ( 2032.310, subd. (a); 2032.320, subd. (e).) Prevailing party to prepare the order. Jim Weixel, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT
